summary: Iteration 6 - Bug Fix Testing: Transaction Edit Save + Calendar Date Picker. Backend PUT endpoint fully working (10/10 tests pass). Frontend edit saves data via API correctly. Date picker is NOT rendering as native calendar picker - it's a text input due to React Native Web behavior.

ui_bugs.issue: Date input renders as type='text' instead of type='date'. The code has <input type='date'> but React Native Web converts it to a TextInput which loses the native calendar picker functionality.
ui_bugs.workaround: User can still manually enter date in YYYY-MM-DD format

action_items: Date picker: The <input type='date'> inside React Native Web is being converted to a TextInput. For native calendar picker on web, need to use a web-specific approach. Options: 1) Use dangerouslySetInnerHTML to inject actual HTML input, 2) Use a date picker library like react-datepicker, 3) Use Expo's DateTimePicker with web polyfill. Current implementation allows manual YYYY-MM-DD entry which works but lacks UX of native picker.
critical_code_review_comments: Line 628-643 in transactions.tsx: The <input type='date'> HTML element is placed inside a React Native View. React Native Web transforms standard HTML elements into RN primitives, which causes the type='date' attribute to be ignored. The element becomes a regular TextInput.
Recommendation: For true native calendar picker on web, consider conditionally rendering outside of RN components or using Platform.select with web-specific date picker library.

success_rate.frontend: 90% (Edit save works, calendar picker UX degraded)

seed_data_creation: Test transactions created via backend tests (prefixed with TEST_)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Transaction edit now calls PUT API and persists correctly. The date field accepts manual YYYY-MM-DD input. For calendar picker, React Native Web limitation - needs web-specific date picker solution. Backend has comprehensive PUT endpoint tests in test_transaction_edit_bug.py.

transaction_edit_not_saving.evidence: PUT /api/transactions/:id returns 200, data persists in database, verified via both backend tests and UI testing

calendar_date_picker.evidence: Code has <input type='date'> but React Native Web transforms it to text input. User can enter date manually but no native calendar popup appears.
calendar_date_picker.recommendation: Use Platform.OS check with web-specific date picker library or HTML injection approach
